If A is ordered (planar), then

• Root: node without parent.
• Intern node: has at least one
child.
• External node (leaf): node with
no children.
• Descendants of a node: children,
grand children, etc.
• Brothers of a node: all other
nodes having the same parent.
• Subtree: some node and all its
descendants.

• “parent” relation
reprezentation: parent
array
• Advantages:
– Simplicity;
– Easy access from any node
to the root;
– Memory saving.

• a node v (stored at address v) is a
structure with three fields:
– v->inf /*information stored in node*/
– v->left  /*left child address*/
– v->right /*right child address*/

procedure preorder(v, visit)
begin
if (v == NULL)
then return
else
visit(v)
preorder(v->left, visit)
preorder(v->right, visit)
end

procedure BFS(t, visit)
begin
if (t == NULL) then return
else
Queue  emptyQueue()
insert(Queue, t)
while (not isEmpty(Queue)) do
read(Queue, v); visit(v)
if (v->left != NULL)
then insert(Queue, v->left)
if (v->right != NULL)
then insert(Queue, v->right)
delete(Queue)
end
